title: Project Report: CodeQuest Reports Catalog App — Frontend React Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: React app fetching catalog.json: search, filter by family, detail view with chapter list, TypeScript throughout.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**CodeQuest Reports Catalog App** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for Frontend React Developer. Duration: 3–4 weeks. React app fetching catalog.json: search, filter by family, detail view with chapter list, TypeScript throughout. Tools: React 19, TypeScript, TanStack Query, Vitest.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Fetch and display catalog.json
Objective: Search and filter by family/level
Objective: Report detail page with chapters
Objective: TanStack Query for caching
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Frontend React Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Frontend React Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Frontend React Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: GitHub repo; Live demo; Test report; Component storybook optional. Skills demonstrated: React, TypeScript, Data fetching. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: GitHub repo
Deliverable: Live demo
Deliverable: Test report
Deliverable: Component storybook optional
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits GitHub repo and passes the rubric.
